Okay, a lot of you are creating lead-generating PDFs, and I think that's great. I would start with a lead-generating PDF. If you want to get more advanced, you can actually create lead-generating video series. This can be everything from a webinar to a free e-course, those kinds of things. But we've had a lot of effective business come off of just these small, kind of free, what feel like video e-courses, and they're really not quite that. For instance, we created five-minute marketing makeover. It was just three five-minute videos, supposedly. The last one is like 10 minutes. But we created three five-minute videos that people could go through, and we gave enormous amounts of great information. Same as the PDF. You want to give enormous amounts of great information. You want to create reciprocity. You want to position yourself as the guide, all those kinds of things. A couple things on videos of this sort. I'd keep them pretty short. I wouldn't go much over 10 minutes unless you have really fantastic content. Keep them pretty short. I like the five-minute marketing makeover because everybody thought, hey, I can get this done in five minutes. It actually took about 20 when you broke it up. People weren't hesitant to go and start it and watch it even while they were sitting there at work. Next thing you want to do is do create a series. Instead of just one video, you might release a video each day in an Evergreen campaign or in a general campaign, or you might just let them have all three videos. I tend to like three. They can have all three videos at once. If they break it up and spend five minutes investing in this, and five minutes investing in this, and then another five minutes investing in this, they feel like they've invested a lot more than just 15 minutes. They've actually gone through a series of thoughts. I like breaking up the content into one very helpful idea. Another very helpful idea, and another very helpful idea. You're breaking it up as, imagine at Christmas, putting three presents into one box and wrapping it and giving it to somebody, or three different presents wrapped individually and giving it to somebody. They're going to perceive more value over the three individually wrapped presents. There's a tip for you for Christmas. You want to do the same thing as you're talking to your customers. It's just really valuable stuff. Also, breaking it up into three or four or five, whatever, allows you to email people three separate times. That's three separate times that your email is coming across their iPhone. Even if they delete it, I love that they're seeing your name, the name of your business in their iPhone. That is incredibly valuable, even if they don't read it. It's three more excuses to email them. One last thing if you're going to create a video series. Make them dovetail into each other. What I mean by that is, as you get to the end of one video, say, all right, that's really great. I hope you guys enjoy that information. The next video I'm going to send you is going to address this problem. It is such a painful problem. It is aggravating you. It's costing you enormous amounts of pain. I'm going to tell you how to fix it in my next video. That gets them to watch the next video. Otherwise, you won't get retention. You'll get 2,000 people watching the first video and 800 people watching the second. When you actually dovetail into the next video, you get 2,000 people watching this. You get 1,800 or 2,000. Sometimes we have more people in our marketing money pit campaign, more people watch the second video than watch the first video. Think like a storyteller. Think like a novelist. The end of that chapter, you don't want to resolve the tension at the end of a chapter. They won't read the next chapter. You want to open up a new story loop. Open up some more tensions so they'll go into the next one. Same with your video series. Even in that third video or the fourth video however long you take it, you want to close the deal. Here's what I mean by that. I like this theory, this philosophy of you give away the why. Here's why you need to eat more healthy. Here's why you need to become a better baseball player. Here's why you need to take strokes off your golf game. Here's why you need to lose weight. Here's why you need to work on your marriage. Here's another reason why. Here's another reason why. In the fourth video or third, depending on how many videos you're going to make, is the sales video. The sales video is here's why. Here's how. If you want to know how and you want me to walk you through it, pay for this product or this service or whatever it is, and I'll walk you through it. Or I'll do it for you. Let's say I'm an HVAC company and I service air conditioners all over Nashville. I want to talk about the first video is not even really about HVAC systems or air conditioning. It's like 10 things I can do to make my home more economical. I need to seal up my windows. I need to do all this kind of stuff. I need to make sure the air conditioner is not running when I'm away. I can get a nest deal and make sure. In that video might be how to save $1,000 a year on your electrical bill. That's the first video. The second video is about the stuff that's in my vents and the pet pollen, whatever that I'm breathing, and you're going to save you from that. I can clean that all myself. The third video is something else that you as an HVAC expert have. The fourth video is for $59.95 will come to a basic service. What have you done? You've given me three really pieces of valuable information. You've given me the name of your company 57 times. You've given me three emails. Am I going to buy the $59.95 thing to maybe have you clean out my ducks for me or install the nest thermostat for me? I may. You're going to have a percentage of people doing that. It's certainly going to pay for that investment. If I don't do that, you've still gotten incredibly value off that marketing because four separate times you've reminded me that you exist. When I'm out mowing the lawn and I turn off my mower and I hear my AC unit over here cranking and making crazy noises, whom am I going to think of? I'm going to think of you because you talked to me four times and you gave me incredibly valuable information. Think similarly to creating a PDF, but it all goes to video. Let's talk for a second about quality. How big is this production? Listen, you can find a video person in your area to do this for a big, giant expense. Might be $5,000. You can probably get this done for about $1,500 edited. We're talking about three, five minute videos. You probably get it done about $1,500 to somebody who can shoot it and do editing for you. Sometimes we bring in multiple cameras. This particular shoot, it's just one camera with some lighting in a great room. People will tell you the quality doesn't have to be good on the internet. I don't like that. I like creating quality for our customers. We go a little bit further and we create some quality, but it's going to be about $1,500. Then you have a designer lay out those landing pages. That may cost you another $1,500. Now you're about $3,000 in. If you've got email addresses, you start hitting your email. You can make that $3,000. Hopefully, you figure out how much your product is and what your profit margins are. You should be getting away with this for about $3,000. We will spend up to $20,000 and highly recommend it if you can. You have the means. You can do that. We could probably get it done for about $5,000, but we go the extra mile and do great sound and all this other kind of stuff. Video is incredibly effective today. You can duplicate it on Facebook and you can promote it on Instagram and all this kind of stuff, but people like these video series. That said, you say, I don't have that kind of money and I look terrible on camera and I don't have anybody who can ... A PDF is almost as effective. I'm not going to lie to you. Having a copywriter create a PDF that generates leads and closes deals is literally almost as effective as a $20,000 video campaign. We just like providing that kind of value for our customers and it certainly pays for itself many, many times over. If you want to take it to the next step, create a video lead generating magnet. Create a video series, put in the content in videos and you should be great. One more tip. All right, let's get to work.
